---
title: "Swamp Smartweed (Persicaria hydropiperoides)"
slug: "persicaria-hydropiperoides"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/355524227/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Swamp Smartweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Caryophyllales"
tax_family: "Polygonaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Caryophyllales", "Polygonaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/355524227/medium.jpeg" alt="Swamp Smartweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">
coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/201404543">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Caryophyllales
-   **Family:** Polygonaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=78453)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches.

## Natural history notes

A wetland plant characterized by its slender stems, lance-shaped leaves, and spikes of small pink to white flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and provides habitat and food resources for a variety of wetland organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/78453-Persicaria-hydropiperoides)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3909)
